page: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. This configuration delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes 35 MB of L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This instance includes 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, offering high-speed local data access. It is optimized for both network and Elastic Block Store (EBS) performance, delivering a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. This high-throughput network interface makes it suitable for data-intensive transfers and clustered environments.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core CPU performance, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores placing in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is also highly efficient, with read-only and write operations on large blocks scoring in the top tier. Database operations and Redis SET operations demonstrate strong performance, executing 74.23 million operations per second. However, single-core CPU tasks and LLM text generation benchmarks fall into average and weak categories, highlighting a tradeoff where parallelized workloads are significantly more efficient than single-threaded execution.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m5dn.24xlarge offers a dense resource profile combining high-speed networking, local NVMe storage, and substantial memory capacity on a single node. This makes it highly suitable for workloads requiring balanced compute and memory paired with fast local storage, such as relational databases, Redis caching layers, high-throughput web serving, and distributed data processing.
description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance powered by a dedicated 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon 8259CL processor. Utilizing the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 96 vCPUs, 48 physical cores, and 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. The instance features 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and excellent database and Redis throughput, though single-core and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This hardware profile is well-suited for high-throughput databases, caching layers, and distributed data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, 3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5dn.24xlarge features 96 vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, 3600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: A high-bandwidth general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, local NVMe storage, and 100 Gbps baseline networking.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 384 GB DDR4 RAM powered by Intel Xeon 8259CL processors.
3600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed local data access.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for high-throughput workloads.
Strong multi-core CPU, database, and Redis benchmark performance.
Best suited for relational databases, caching layers, and distributed data processing.
